history.summary: Revenue Control redesign, outstanding payments popup, future revenue fix.
changes: Revenue Control page simplified — cleaner layout with KPI cards only
Outstanding Payments moved to a popup — click the Owed cards to view and manage unpaid appointments
Owed KPI cards show a visual hint (click to view) so you know they are interactive
Future Revenue section cleaned up — shows summary KPIs without the appointment list
Fixed revenue reports not loading on local/development database

history.summary: Sticky calendar view, mark as paid on creation, searchable patient selector sorted by last appointment.
changes: Calendar view (Week/Month) remembers your choice — stays on your last selection when navigating away and coming back
Your calendar view preference is saved for next time you log in
You can now mark an appointment as paid right when you create it
Payment option is now visible for all users when creating or editing appointments
Type to search for a patient by name when creating an appointment
Patient list sorted by most recent appointment — patients you saw recently appear at the top
Each patient shows when their last appointment was (e.g. Last: yesterday, Last: 3 days ago)

history.summary: Patient NIF and insurance, therapist-scoped patient visibility, duplicate detection, admin financial data restriction.
changes: Patient NIF (fiscal identification) field with mod-11 check digit validation (frontend + backend)
Patient insurance management — CRUD for insurance name, policy number (max 35 chars), and provider
Insurance provider selector with ADSE preset and free-text option for other providers
Patient visibility restricted by therapist — each therapist only sees patients they registered
Admins see all patients across all therapists
Ownership enforcement on patient updates — therapists can only edit their own patients
Duplicate patient detection for admins — warns on matching NIF or identical name across therapists
Duplicate warning banner on Patient Registry with expandable details
NIF and insurance displayed in patient appointment history info card
Admins can no longer view financial data — fee amounts, paid/owed status, balances, and payment types are hidden
Payment filters and payment-related form fields hidden from admin in Appointment Management
Schedule calendar chips no longer show paid/owed colour coding for admins

history.summary: Password strength meter, theme-aware logo and login, session cookie fix, better-sqlite3 migration.
changes: Password strength indicator on all password fields (new user, edit user, profile)
SVG logo uses currentColor — follows the active theme colour
Default CSS theme is now light-blue; login screen no longer appears green
Session cookie uses secure:auto — works on both HTTP (Docker) and HTTPS (Vercel)
Replaced sqlite3 with better-sqlite3 — prebuilt binaries, no native compilation needed
Clock display simplified — removed seconds, updates every 60s

history.summary: Therapist selector for admins, Vercel session persistence, Revenue Control fixes, last login tracking.
changes: Therapist selector in Quick Add Appointment — admins can assign a therapist when creating appointments
PostgreSQL session store via connect-pg-simple — sessions persist across Vercel serverless invocations
Revenue Control reports fixed for PostgreSQL — replaced SQLite strftime() with PG-compatible to_char()
ensureAuth no longer redirects to login on page-level API errors
Last login tracking — login timestamp recorded and displayed in User Management table
NeonDB connection using @neondatabase/serverless WebSocket Pool driver for Vercel
Express routes registered synchronously for Vercel serverless compatibility
Fixed PostgreSQL parameter placeholder bugs in appointment update queries
Cleaned up duplicate build-info routes in auth module

history.summary: PostgreSQL session persistence, build counter, production-ready Vercel deployment.
changes: PostgreSQL session store for Vercel serverless — sessions now persist across function instances
Build counter implementation — version displays as v2.1.X for tracking deployments
Environment indicator (DEV: SQLite / PROD: NeonDB) displayed in bottom-right corner
Auto-detection of DATABASE_URL for production database switching
/ALTApi/auth/build-info endpoint exposes build counter to frontend
Session cookie configuration with HTTPS auto-detection for production
Connect-pg-simple integration for safe serverless session storage
Fallback to memory store if PostgreSQL unavailable (graceful degradation)

history.summary: Session persistence fixes, lazy database loading, API restructuring, favicon.
changes: Fixed session persistence issue by including credentials in fetch requests
Implemented lazy loading of database module across all route handlers
Fixed SQL syntax for SQLite in reports endpoints (monthly, yearly, total, future)
Changed API URL structure from /api/ to /ALTApi/ for all endpoints
Made index.html the landing page for better user experience
Added SVG favicon to all HTML pages for branded browser tab appearance
Consolidated database connections for improved reliability
Ensured session authentication persists across page navigation

history.summary: Theme system, per-user theme persistence, UI refinements.
changes: 4 colour themes — light-green, light-blue, dark-green, dark-blue with full CSS variable system
Per-user theme persistence — theme saved to database via API, loaded on login
Theme picker with colour swatches in the user status bar
Admin badge (red ADMIN pill) displayed next to admin usernames
Dark mode support with dedicated CSS overrides
Distinctive logout button — solid red background across all themes
About link right-aligned in navigation bar
About page shows only current version with expandable version history toggle

history.summary: UX polish, patient addresses, future payment blocking, date clock, seeded data.
changes: Future appointments cannot be marked as paid — enforced on backend (POST, PUT, PATCH) and frontend
Patient address field — stored per patient, defaults into appointment address, editable per appointment
Google Maps links (📍) next to all address fields across appointments and patient views
User status bar with live clock showing date and time, profile link, and logout button
Patient listing table simplified — removed condition column from table view
Test data seeded — 10 patients with randomised appointments for development
Payment type tracking (Wire, MBWay, Cash, etc.)
Patient comments section with date-tagged notes
Appointment history panel within patient details

history.summary: First tagged release — full multi-user system with role-based access control.
changes: Admin appointment management — admin sees all appointments, filters by therapist, schedules for therapists, edits any appointment and can reassign therapists
Therapist endpoint — new /api/users/therapists for populating therapist dropdowns across the app
Revenue Control therapist filter — admin can filter all revenue sections (total, monthly, future, outstanding) by therapist; non-admin users see only their own data
Report endpoints accept userId parameter — /api/reports/total, /monthly, /yearly and /future all support admin filtering by therapist
Admin-only navigation — Configuration and User Management links hidden from non-admin users via data-admin-only attribute
Settings page access control — non-admin users who navigate directly to /settings.html see Access Denied
Nav hidden before login — main navigation bar and logout button are hidden until authenticated
Simplified role-based UI — single updateNavForRole() function using data-admin-only for all admin elements

history.summary: Multi-user RBAC with block/unblock, user management, profile page.
changes: Multi-user authentication system with username and password (bcrypt)
Role-based access control — admin and therapist roles
User Management page (admin-only) — create, edit, block/unblock users
My Profile page — self-service profile and password changes
Case-insensitive login
Blocked user rejection at login
All pages updated with username+password login forms
Shared common.js with AppCommon utility (login, session, nav role management)
Patient Registry with comments section and appointment history
Revenue Control with total, monthly, future, and outstanding payments
Appointment Management with filters, search, and pagination
Schedule Hub with weekly and monthly calendar views
Configuration page for default fee settings
Payment confirmation dialog
Payment type tracking (Wire, MBWay, Cash, etc.)
